stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 energy source options. The probability of favorable regional climate data is uncertain, estimated between 42% and 55%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: solar: $290K in good regional climate data, $77K in bad regional climate data. wind: $249K in good regional climate data, $105K in bad regional climate data. natural gas: $251K in good regional climate data, $28K in bad regional climate data. geothermal: $229K in good regional climate data, $70K in bad regional climate data. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.48: best option is solar with EV=$179.2K. solar is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [166.5, 194.2].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
solar is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
Not performing minimax regret analysis to find the most robust choice
